content.full_text:
October 1, 1990

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Informal Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter is written to request informal advice from the Fair Political Practices Commission pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) and 2 Cal. Code of Regs. 18329 on behalf of the California Police Chiefs' Association.

The question posed to the Commission is as follows: Under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, may a local police department enter into contracts for required police training services with Company X where Company X has been a source of $250 or more income to the Chief of Police instructing a State certified and reimbursed training course within the last 12 months?

More specifically, for the purpose of this request, the facts are as follows: Company X is a certified provider for continuing education and required police training, authorized and reimbursed by the Department of Justice Commission on Peace Officer Standards and Training (P.O.S.T.). The Police Chief, a court qualified training expert and one of two instructors certified by the state to teach this specific P.O.S.T. reimbursed course, was employed by Company X within the last 12 months to teach the course at a P.O.S.T. determined rate of pay. Company X is certified by P.O.S.T. to exclusively provide this course, which is taught only by the Chief and the other instructor. It should be noted the Chief is not directly involved in the selection or assignment of training courses for his department. This is delegated through the chain of command to the Training Unit.

Company X has also contracted with the Police Department in the past to provide training and promotional testing prior to the current Chief's administration. The scope of Company X's involvement with the City in providing contemplated future courses could include both P.O.S.T. certified/reimbursable courses as well as training not to be reimbursed to the department by the state. Under no circumstances would the Chief be employed for any non-certified/reimbursed training nor would the Chief be involved in any decision making effecting Company X.

Page 2

Many P.O.S.T. courses require the exclusive expertise of police
chiefs, which is not commonly available in the private sector.
It should be noted that there is past practice in this matter state-
wide.

Your prompt attention in rendering an opinion would be most
appreciated as Association members comprise a significant number
of the instructors for P.O.S.T. approved peace officer training
courses. The Association's objective is to properly advise its members
as to this matter so as to avoid potential conflicts of interest.

Please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned if you have any
questions about this request.

Very truly yours,

Donald J. Burnett
President/

cc: Larry Todd, Chairman
     C.P.C.A. Standards and Ethics Committee
